verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP/GRP78) is a classical ER lumen chaperone protein. The NCBI summary explicitly states it 'localizes to the lumen of the endoplasmic reticulum (ER)' where it functions as an HSP70 chaperone. While it interacts with transmembrane stress sensors (PERK, IRE1, ATF6) at the ER membrane during stress, these are interactions with the cytoplasmic/luminal faces of ER transmembrane proteins — not surface exposure via the protein's own mechanism. The protein itself resides in the ER lumen and does not have a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, lipid modification, or any documented outer-leaflet PM localization. No therapeutic programs target HSPA5 on the cell surface. The protein is endomembrane-resident.
